paper_title: Coherent-State Propagation: A Computational Framework for Simulating Bosonic Quantum Systems

perturbations.why_wrong: The runtime exponent $L$ was moved from a power to a multiplicative factor. This contradicts the subsequent claim that the simulation is quasi-polynomial ($m^{\mathcal{O}(\log m)}$) for $L=\mathcal{O}(\log m)$, as a multiplicative $L$ would result in polynomial time.

The assumption on $\kappa$ was changed from $1/(L(1+\lambda^2))$ to $1/(L(1+\lambda))$. This is inconsistent with the later Theorem 7 (S0007) which explicitly defines the small-nonlinearity regime scaling as $1/(L\lambda^2)$ for $\lambda > 1$.

The first term of the improved cost for coherent states was changed from $N^2 m$ to $N m$. This is incorrect because computing the $N^2$ overlaps between $N$ components in the mixture $q(x)$ (as defined in Theorem 9) requires $N^2$ operations even for product states.

The cost for coherent-product states was changed from $\eta + q_{\max}s_{\max}$ to $\eta + q_{\max} + s_{\max}$. This is incorrect because the expectation value of a monomial $\hat X^q \hat P^s$ involves the product of the powers when expanding in the coherent state basis.

The phase factor in the definition of $c_k$ was changed from $e^{i\kappa n^2}$ to $e^{i\kappa n}$. This is incorrect because the Kerr gate $K_\kappa$ is defined as $\exp(i\kappa \hat n^2)$, which acts on Fock states $|n\rangle$ as $e^{i\kappa n^2}|n\rangle$.

The term $(A_{\rm in}+\Gamma)\sqrt{\log(L/\varepsilon)}$ in the definition of $N_F$ was changed to $(A_{\rm in}+\Gamma)\log(L/\varepsilon)$. This is inconsistent with the context immediately preceding the theorem, which shows the square root dependence in the bracketed expression for $\alpha_{\max}$.

The sign in the second term was changed from minus to plus: $(\lambda^2+\lambda)+e^{-2\lambda}(\lambda^2-\lambda)$. This is inconsistent with the definition of $f(\lambda)$ in Theorem 7 (S0007), which is derived from this bound and uses a minus sign in the numerator.

The exponent in the error bound was changed from $s+1$ to $s$. This is incorrect because the truncation error for a sum of $s$ terms in a Taylor-like expansion (or binomial expansion) typically scales with the first neglected term, which is the $(s+1)$-th power.

The TV distance bound was changed from $(1-b)^t$ to $(1-b)t$. This is incorrect as it would imply the error increases with time $t$ (since $b$ is small), whereas the subsequent line correctly shows an exponential decay $\exp(-t/M)$.
The bound $P(x) \le N q(x)$ was changed to $P(x) \le N^2 q(x)$. This is internally inconsistent with the very next line in the same theorem, which states the ratio $P(x)/q(x) \le N$.
